PhysicsWallah – Some insights based on concalls Q3

Business Strengths
• Zero CAC model: Free YouTube content drives paid users → very low marketing cost (~8–9%).
• Massive distribution: 250+ YouTube channels, ~3.4M daily active users.
• High engagement: Students spend ~106 minutes daily on the app.
• Strong growth: Revenue growing ~30–35% annually.
• Strong cash: ~₹5,000 Cr treasury after IPO for expansion and acquisitions.

Growth Drivers
• K-12 expansion: Much larger market than test prep; could become biggest segment in 5–7 years.
• Offline expansion: 300+ centers with plans to add more.
• New exam categories: CLAT, NEET PG, IIT JAM, state boards etc.
• Regional language courses: Expanding into southern and vernacular markets.
• AI integration: Proprietary AI models (Aryabhata) for personalized learning.

Key Strategic Advantage
• Community-led growth: YouTube → App → Paid batches funnel is hard for competitors to replicate.

Major Risks
• Dependence on test-prep: JEE/NEET growth slowing in mature regions.
• Execution risk: K-12 schools, AI products, and acquisitions are new businesses.
• High investment phase: Expansion could pressure margins in near term.
• Competition: Global AI/edtech platforms entering education.
• Regulation risk: Coaching and school sectors may face policy changes.

Overall Conclusion
• Market is valuing PW not as a coaching company but as a long-term education platform.
• Future success depends on scaling K-12 + AI while maintaining strong growth in core test-prep.

Jio IPO Delayed Amid Regulatory Uncertainty

The IPO of Reliance Jio Platforms has been delayed due to pending government approvals on listing rule changes.

Reliance is waiting for the government to finalise regulatory amendments proposed by SEBI before filing its draft IPO prospectus.

SEBI earlier approved changes allowing companies with post-issue market capitalisation above ₹5 lakh crore to dilute only 2.5% stake in an IPO, instead of the current 5% requirement.

The rule change could facilitate mega listings like Jio and NSE, but it still awaits final government notification.

Reliance was targeting a Jio listing in early 2026, but the timeline may shift depending on regulatory clarity.

Investment bankers estimate the IPO could value Jio Platforms at around $170 billion.

The listing would be the first IPO of a major Reliance unit in nearly two decades and could become India’s biggest-ever IPO.

Jio has attracted over $10 billion in investments from global players like Meta and Alphabet since 2020.

AI Data Centres Don't Need Wires & Cables Now

Nvidia is moving AI data centers from copper cables wires to optics… here is why it could have repercussions for growth of Indian Wires & Cables players like Polycab, KEI etc.

Context 
Jensen Huang’s Nvidia recently committed $4B to optical ecosystem players like Coherent Corp. and Lumentum Holdings to secure silicon photonics, lasers and optical networking for next-gen AI data centers.

Why? Because copper cables are hitting limits in dense AI GPU clusters:
• Too much heat & power consumption
• Bandwidth constraints
• Physical bulk blocking airflow in racks

So hyperscale AI clusters are increasingly shifting to optical interconnects (fiber + photonics) instead of copper.

Meanwhile in India, Polycab, KEI & the entire cables & wires sector is being pitched as a pure "AI & Data Centre proxy."

KEI Industries CMD Anil Gupta called data centres a "₹25,000 crore opportunity for the cables industry" by 2030-31.

Motilal Oswal's sector note projects Indian C&W companies growing at 17% revenue CAGR over FY26–28. Key drivers cited: real estate, renewables, infra, industrial capex.

Data centres? A "powerful, long-duration driver" — but nowhere close to being the primary revenue engine today.

Even if all that plays out, the absolute cable demand from data centers remains relatively small compared to overall industry demand from housing, power, infrastructure etc.

So the real question:

Is the market overestimating the “AI/Data Center” opportunity for Indian Wires & Cables — especially when AI infrastructure itself is moving toward optics rather than copper

**The NAV Trap: Why Your “Same Day” Mutual Fund Order May Fail**

**The Reality Behind 2 PM Cutoff**
• Many investors assume placing an order before **2:00 PM** guarantees the **same day NAV**
• In reality, clicking **“Pay” at 1:45 PM** does **not guarantee** today’s NAV
• The key factor is **when the money is actually received by the Clearing Corporation**
• If funds reach after the cutoff, investors get **next day NAV**

**What Actually Happens in the System**
• Payments go through **aggregators** like **BillDesk** or **Razorpay**
• These platforms process and forward the transaction to **ICCL (Indian Clearing Corporation Ltd)**
• Even if payment is instant, **reporting delay can happen**
• Example case
• Payment completed at **13:38:13**
• Reported to clearing system at **14:32:31**
• Result: **Next Day NAV Allotment**

**Important Rule Investors Must Understand**
• NAV allotment depends on **fund realization**
• This means the time when **AMC actually receives usable funds**
• If realization happens after **2:00 PM**, order shifts to **next day NAV**
• Execution time and realization time are **two different things**

**How Smart Investors Avoid This Trap**
**Buffer Time Strategy**
• Place orders **before 1:00 PM**
• This gives buffer for **payment gateway delays**
• Helps secure **same day NAV**

**Choose the Right Payment Mode**
• Prefer **UPI or Net Banking**
• Best reliability from major banks
• **HDFC Bank**
• **ICICI Bank**
• **SBI**
• **Axis Bank**
• **Kotak Bank**
• These usually process funds **faster to clearing systems**

**Payment Modes to Avoid for Same-Day NAV**
• **NEFT**
• **RTGS**
• **Mandates / Auto debit**
• These methods may take **T+1 or even T+2** to reflect

**Investor Insight: Market Timing Mistake**
• Many investors wait for **last minute market dips**
• Large number of transactions create **payment congestion**
• Aggregator delays increase during **high volume periods**
• Late orders often lead to **unexpected next day NAV**

**Key Takeaway for Investors**
• Do not rely on the **2 PM cutoff myth**
• The real cutoff is **fund realization time**
• Always place orders **well before 1 PM**
• Early execution ensures **NAV certainty and better investment discipline**

**Small/Midcap Funds in 18-Month Drawdown**

Markets down since Sep 26, 2024 (~17-18 months to Mar 2026)—classic correction for long-term entry.  

**Index Performance**  
Nifty Smallcap 100: Recent levels ~19,000 (52-wk high 19,225; low 14,084); recent 1-mo volatility -3-4% avg, 3-mo -7.5%.  
Nifty Midcap 100: ~57,000 (recent 3-mo swings -8% to +1%).  

**Fund Resilience**  
Top mid/smallcap funds show 1-yr returns 18-27% despite correction; 3-yr 24-25% annualized. Leaders: Mirae Midcap (-3.4% 1-mo, 18% 1-yr); HDFC/Nippon midcaps 19-21% 1-yr.  

**Historical Opportunity**  
Corrections like this (post-2023 peaks) averaged -20-30% drawdowns but delivered 50%+ rebounds over 3 yrs for quality funds.  

**Strategy Tip**  
Ignore short-term winners; focus 3+ yr horizon, quality managers, diversified SIPs. Due diligence essential.  

**Key Takeaway**  
Prolonged drawdowns forge long-term wealth—small/midcap funds shine post-correction for patient investors.

**India’s Mutual Fund AUM Surge**

India’s MF AUM reached ₹80.8 lakh crore in Jan 2026, up 20% YoY (+₹13-14 lakh Cr added).  

**Inflow Breakdown**  
- Equity: ₹3.7 lakh Cr  
- Debt: ₹1.9 lakh Cr  
- Hybrid: ₹1.7 lakh Cr  

**Equity Allocation Shift**  
Large caps >32%; midcaps expanding; smallcaps stable.  

**Alternatives Boom**  
Gold AUM +257% (record ETF inflows); silver, overseas, REITs up sharply. Debt shortens for safety.  

**Maturing Behaviour**  
SIP explosion, diversification, less FD reliance—aggressive yet smart allocation.  

**Key Takeaway**  
Retail India shifts to multi-asset growth via MFs, signaling wealth creation maturity.

**Global Oil Chokepoints 🛢️**

​Nearly **92% of global maritime oil** passes through just a few narrow gateways. The **Strait of Malacca** and **Strait of Hormuz** are the heavyweights, together handling over 55% of the world’s sea-traded oil.

​From the Suez Canal to the Cape of Good Hope, these routes are the arteries of the global economy. Any disruption at these "chokepoints" doesn't just stall ships—it risks global energy security and sends prices skyrocketing.

**The Sweet Spot of Iranian Crude Oil**

Iranian Light crude, with its 33-36° API gravity and 1.36-1.5% sulfur content, hits the perfect balance for global refineries. It yields high-value products like gasoline and diesel with minimal processing—unlike heavier Venezuelan Merey (16° API) or lighter US WTI (39-40° API). Despite sanctions, demand persists via Dubai networks, as refineries optimized for it struggle with substitutes. Amid 2026 Strait of Hormuz tensions, prices have spiked to $82 per barrel, highlighting its enduring appeal.

GNFC: LNG Supply Disruption Forces Cut in Gas Allocation

• ⚠️ Force Majeure Triggered: LNG supply disruption due to Middle East conflict impacting Petronet LNG shipments.

• 🔗 Supply Chain Impact: GAIL receives Force Majeure notice from Petronet LNG, and passes it on to GNFC.

• ⛽ Gas Allocation Reduced: RLNG supply to GNFC cut to 60% of Daily Contracted Quantity (DCQ) from March 6, 2026.

• 🏭 Production Impact: Neem Urea production likely to be affected, while other products remain unaffected for now.

• 📊 Uncertain Duration: Company says impact cannot yet be quantified as the situation is ongoing.

🔴 Impact: Negative
Reduced gas supply may disrupt fertiliser production and impact near-term operations.

**Flexi Cap Funds: Active vs Patient Portfolios**

**Churn Spectrum**  
Flexi Cap funds vary sharply in portfolio turnover. High churners like Quant Flexi Cap trade aggressively for alpha. Patient players like Kotak Flexi Cap and PPFAS Flexi Cap hold conviction bets longer.  

**Key Examples**  
- **Quant Flexi Cap**: High turnover style; dynamic shifts across market caps for momentum plays.  
- **Kotak Flexi Cap**: Low turnover (~9%); 59 stocks, large/mid bias. Top holdings: HDFC Bank, BEL.  
- **PPFAS Flexi Cap**: Equity turnover ~14%; focuses long-term compounding with global diversification.  

**Investor Implications**  
High churn boosts short-term returns but racks up costs/taxes. Low turnover favors tax efficiency, lower expenses, steady growth. Category average exceeds 200%.  

**Key Takeaway**  
Match fund style to your horizon—aggressive for tactical edges, patient for buy-and-hold wealth building.

reports.text:
Max Financial Services : Co Considers Amalgamation with Axis Max Life Insurance

• 🔄 Proposed Amalgamation: Max Financial Services and Axis Max Life Insurance (AMLI) are evaluating a merger proposal, subject to regulatory approvals including IRDAI.

• 🏦 Axis Entities Stake: Axis Bank group entities hold ~19.02% stake in AMLI, while Max Financial holds ~80.98%.

• 📈 Listing Plan: AMLI may pursue stock market listing without an IPO, or explore IPO/other value-unlocking options.

• 🤝 Axis Group Approval: Axis Bank, Axis Securities and Axis Capital have provided in-principle no-objection (Axis NOC) for the proposed amalgamation.

• ⚙️ Alternative Options: If amalgamation is not completed, exit sale, third-party sale or forced IPO routes may be considered.

🟢 Impact: Positive
Potential value unlocking and listing of the insurance business could enhance shareholder value and improve transparency.

reports.text:
Rising Oil Prices May Push India’s Inflation & Currency Pressure

• 📈 Inflation Impact: A 10% rise in global crude prices may increase India’s WPI inflation by ~0.7–1%, according to a Bank of Baroda report.

• 💰 Import Bill Surge: Higher crude prices could raise India’s oil import bill by ~$18 billion (~0.5% of GDP).

• 💱 Rupee Pressure: The ₹ may weaken to 91–92 per $, with risk of crossing ₹92 if tensions persist.

• 🏦 OMCs Absorbing Costs: Oil Marketing Companies may absorb higher crude costs initially, delaying pass-through to consumers.

• 📊 Macro Impact: Higher crude may widen current account deficit and increase subsidy burden (fuel & fertilisers).

🔴 Impact: Negative
Higher oil prices increase inflation risk, fiscal pressure, and rupee depreciation, which can weigh on markets and the economy.

Kalpataru Projects International Limited Subsidiary Files for Judicial Reorganization in Brazil

• ⚖️ Restructuring Filed: Fasttel Engenharia S.A., a step-down subsidiary of Kalpataru Projects, filed for Judicial Reorganization (RJ) in Brazil.

• 🏗️ Purpose of RJ: A restructuring process where the company submits a recovery plan to creditors to ensure business continuity.

• 📊 Shareholding Unchanged: No change in Kalpataru’s ownership stake in Fasttel at this stage.

• 🔍 Financial Impact Review: Company is assessing financial exposure and potential provisions related to Fasttel.

🔴 Impact: Negative
Subsidiary entering restructuring signals financial stress in overseas operations, which could lead to provisions or earnings impact depending on exposure.

**COAL INDIA: BUY UPGRADE → TP ₹500 (12% Upside)**  

**Rating & Valuation**  
BUY (upgraded from Hold); 6x FY28E EV/EBITDA vs current 5.5x  
Forward PER ~9x FY28E EPS ₹56.9  

**Macro Tailwinds**  
- Middle East tensions spike global coal prices (Russia-Ukraine 2022 redux)  
- E-auction premiums 55-65% (vs 329% Q2FY23 peak) → 80%+ upside potential  
- Indonesia exports miss CY25 targets → India demand diverts to CIL  
- Nat gas prices rise (Hormuz risks) → coal substitution accelerates  

**Power Demand Revival**  
- FY26 subdued YoY (La Niña + extended monsoon)  
- ENSO → neutral; 40% El Niño odds H2'26  
- Historical El Niño: 5-8% power growth favors coal gen  

**Financial Projections**  
FY25A sales ₹1,41k Cr → FY28E ₹1,61k Cr  
EBITDA ₹37k Cr → ₹44k Cr (26% → 27% margin)  
PAT margin 22% → 24%; ROE 39% → 25%; Div yield 5-6%  

**Strategic Catalysts**  
- CMPDI listing Mar'26; MCL/SECL by Dec'26 (value unlock)  
- 1B tonne production trajectory  
- Jul'26 wage revision sensitivity (10-15% hike risk)  

**Q3 FY26 Snapshot**  
PAT -16% YoY to ₹7.2k Cr (wage provisions); sales flat  
3rd interim dividend ₹5.50/share (Feb'26)  

**Scenario Analysis**  
Bear: -5% vol + power miss + wage hike → FY28E EPS ₹45  
Bull: 90% e-auction +8% vol → FY28E EPS ₹68 (TP ₹620)  

**Risks**  
Weak offtake, power demand miss, aggressive wage hikes  

**Key Takeaway**  
Geopolitics + Indo supply squeeze + El Niño power surge = CIL volume-price re-rating at cheap multiples

Tech-native omnichannel platform focused on design-driven studded jewellery (2/3 revenue).

**Omnichannel Edge**  
Digital drives 70-90% purchases ('Try Home', AI personalization); repeat 44-54%.  
Loyalty via 'Gold Mine', old gold exchange; millennials/GenZ target.

Vs CaratLane: In-house prod vs outsourcing; COCO shift from franchise.  
7,400+ designs via 3D printing; data optimizes inventory/location.  

**Financials FY25**  
Revenue ₹1,770 Cr; Gross margin 37.9%; EBITDA 4.2% (₹75 Cr).  

**Risks**  
Gold volatility, expansion delays, competition (Titan/Kalyan), capex intensity.  

**Key Takeaway:**  
Bluestone captures studded/lifestyle shift with omnichannel scale and vertical integration—inflection to profitability ahead.

**Nuvama IT Sector: Gen AI Update**  

**Disruption Parallel**  
Gen AI mirrors RIMS/digital waves; Indian IT partners platforms (OpenAI, Microsoft, AWS) rather than displaced.  

**Adoption Phases**  
- Brownfield (80-85% enterprise tech): Phased PoCs in legacy contracts.  
- Greenfield: Aggressive on AI-native stacks; now in RFPs/renewals.  

**Pricing & Productivity**  
- AI work +30-40% billing premium vs legacy.  
- Efficiency gains 15-18% avg (up to 35%); token costs drive opex preference.  

**Workforce Shift**  
Gradual reskilling to AI engineers/specialists; billing includes AI agents.  
Agents shrink teams/RCA time (e.g., cyber: 5-6 eng → smaller with AI).  

**Sector Dynamics**  
Regulated sectors (insurance/healthcare) slower due to compliance.  
TAM expands long-term; Indian IT strengthens despite near-term volatility.  

**Key Takeaway:**  
Gen AI boosts IT services pricing/productivity; Indian firms well-positioned as integration partners—near-term demand choppy but structural positive.

**JM Financial on Reliance Industries**  

**Rating & Target**  
- **BUY** maintained; TP ₹1,730 (~29% upside from ₹1,345).  
- Trades near bear-case ₹1,275 after 8% correction; FY28E P/E 16.8x (vs 3-yr avg 23.9x).  

**Correction Overdone**  
- Crude/LNG spike aids O2C: Diesel cracks USD 35-42/bbl (+GRM USD 4-5/bbl potential).  
- Petchem benefits as product prices > feedstock costs (25% crude-linked).  

**Growth Drivers**  
- 14-16% EPS CAGR 3-5 yrs led by Jio (15-16% EBITDA, 10-11% ARPU CAGR); steady Retail.  
- Capex moderates to ₹1.2-1.4 tn/yr, fully funded internally; Net D/E 0.6x.  

**SOTP Breakdown (TP ₹1,730)**  
- Energy ₹514; Digital (Jio) ₹673; Retail ₹545; New Energy ₹111; Others ₹48.  

**Key Risks**  
ARPU/sub weakness, Retail EBITDA drag (quick commerce), O2C softness.

RIL dip offers entry amid O2C tailwinds and Jio growth; undervalued vs historical multiples.

**Motilal Oswal on Jain Resource Recycling**  

**Rating & Target**  
- **BUY** initiation; TP ₹520 (29% upside from ₹403).  
- 21x FY28E EPS (₹24.4); 25% discount to 3-yr peer avg.  

**Business Profile**  
India's largest non-ferrous recycler: Lead (44% revenue), Copper (50%), Aluminium (4%).  
Integrated 287K MT capacity; scrap from 120+ countries, exports to 20+.  

**Industry Tailwinds**  
Secondary lead demand +6.5% CAGR FY24-30; copper +14% CAGR.  
BWMR/GST RCM/EPR rules shift 60% unorg. share to organized players.  

**Lead Growth**  
Volumes +32% CAGR FY22-25 (152-155K MT); capacity 184→300K MT by FY28.  
Value-add: tin/antimony extraction (3QFY27 plant). EBITDA/MT ₹16.9k→₹21k.  

**Copper Growth**  
Revenue/volume +22%/15% CAGR; capacity 83→100K TPA by FY28.  
Forward integration (anodes/cathodes); US JV (25K TPA, Jun'26 start). EBITDA/MT ₹33k→₹48k.  

**Financials FY26-28E**  
Sales ₹93→163 bn; EBITDA ₹6.6→11.8 bn; PAT ₹4.4→8.4 bn.

Deregulation and simplified licensing may improve pricing flexibility and ease of doing business for liquor manufacturers.

reports.text:
For companies above ₹1 lakh crore, the median drawdown was 32% on April 7th 2025. That has since narrowed to below 20%. Similar patterns hold as you move down through the ₹50,000 crore and ₹10,000 crore buckets - the pain peaked and has been receding since 7th April 2025.

But what about below ₹3,000 crore mcap segment?

There has been no recovery. In fact, since April 7th, these stocks have fallen another 3-5%. The median drawdown in the sub-₹3,000 crore universe now sits at around 60% from peak. While the rest of the market has been healing, the segment where we operate has been bleeding further.

ED raids Mumbai, Chennai firms in alleged fraud involving ‘unlisted NSE shares

The ED has conducted searches at eight locations in Mumbai and Chennai as part of a money laundering probe into an alleged investment fraud involving the sale of so-called “unlisted” shares of NSE India Ltd.

The action targeted entities including Atum Capital Pvt Ltd, Optimus Financial Solutions Pvt Ltd, Babli Investment Pvt Ltd and Supremus Angel, along with their directors.

Among those named in the probe are Satish Kumar, Sanjay Damani, Neeraj Nisar, Krish Vohra, Manish Soni and Nisha Kumari, who the agency alleges were part of a cartel that duped investors by promising allocations in unlisted shares of the NSE.

The accused lured investors by claiming they owned shares of NSE India Ltd and could transfer them at a premium through private share purchase agreements. However, the group did not actually possess the shares they were offering. The accused exploited this lack of formal oversight in private transactions to collect large sums from investors as advance payments.

The money raised from investors was routed through multiple bank accounts and allegedly diverted into movable and immovable assets. During the searches, officials seized documents related to properties, along with digital records and other material that the agency described as incriminating.

The ED has since frozen several bank and demat accounts linked to Satish Kumar, Sanjay Damani and the entities under investigation. The agency said evidence of “proceeds of crime” had been detected in these accounts.

Further investigation by the ED into the case is in progress.

India - Downgrade to EW

CLSA on Tyre Cos

Current geopolitical conflict in the Middle East has driven a 15–20% spike in key raw‑material components for the tyre industry in recent months

This poses a significant profitability risk for tyre manufacturers due to:
(1) higher crude‑oil prices—~45% of the raw‑material basket (RMB) is crude‑linked;
(2) rising natural‑rubber prices—another ~45% of the RMB;
(3) INR depreciation against the USD, which further inflates natural/synthetic‑rubber import costs.

If Brent remains around US$80/bbl and domestic natural rubber stays near Rs220/kg for the next three to six months, estimate a 400bps gross‑margin hit for Indian tyre makers in FY27, even after assuming a staggered 4% price hike in the replacement market and full pass‑through in OEMs

Believe, with tyre companies either initiating capex cycles in FY27 or focusing on post‑acquisition deleveraging, this margin compression is likely to pressure FCF generation, capital structure, and near‑term valuation multiples.

Medical tourists account for 6-9% of revenues for large hospitals in India, of which c.10% are from the Middle East, c.15% from Africa and c.7% from Central and Eastern Europe.

Bangladesh accounts for 55-60% of medical tourists, which we believe is unlikely to be impacted by Iran-Israel-US conflict.

Thus, estimate risk to Ebitda for large hospital companies is limited to 3-6% (annualised).

Nonetheless, stocks are down 4-5% and appear to be pricing in the impact on full-year earnings

Gulf Nations Review Investments as War Strains Budgets - FT

• 🛢 Economic Pressure: Major Gulf economies — Saudi Arabia, United Arab Emirates, Kuwait, and Qatar — are reviewing budgets due to war-related strains.

• ⚠️ Force Majeure Review: Governments are examining force majeure clauses in contracts and reassessing current and future investment commitments.

• 📉 Revenue Impact: Budget pressure stems from reduced energy income, shipping disruptions, and slowdown in tourism and aviation.

• 💰 Investment Risk: Gulf sovereign funds, which manage hundreds of billions of dollars in global investments, may reconsider overseas investments.

• 🚢 Shipping Disruption: Conflict has slowed tanker movement through the Strait of Hormuz, a key route for ~20% of global oil and gas trade.

🔴 Impact: Negative
Geopolitical tensions and potential investment pullback by Gulf sovereign funds could increase volatility in global markets and energy supply chains.

**UBS Global Research – India Banks Energy Shock Scenario (5 Mar 2026)**

**Macro Impact – Oil Price Shock**

• UBS analysed impact of rising oil prices due to **Middle East conflict**
• **+10% oil price rise → ~30 bps CPI increase**
• **GDP growth may decline ~15 bps**

• During **Russia–Ukraine war (2022)**
• Oil jumped **$10–30 per barrel for ~6 months**
• Inflation rose **~80–90 bps**
• RBI raised **Repo Rate ~150 bps**

**Scenario 1 – Oil Price Rise < $10 per Barrel**

• **EPS Impact: +1% to −3% (Limited impact)**

• **Loan Growth:** Stable due to **higher SME working capital demand**
• **NIM:** Stable as **rate cut cycle may end**
• **Non-interest income:** Slight decline due to **treasury losses**
• **Credit costs:** **Minimal asset quality impact**

• **Preferred Banks:**
• **Axis Bank**
• **Kotak Mahindra Bank**

**Scenario 2 – Oil Price Rise $20–30 per Barrel**

• **EPS Impact: −4% to −8% (Sector pressure)**

• **Loan Growth:** Slight slowdown due to **consumption weakness**
• **NIM:** **0 to −5 bps decline** due to loan mix change
• **Non-interest income:** Decline due to **lower consumption and treasury losses**
• **Credit costs:** **0 to +5 bps increase**

• **Preferred Bank:** **ICICI Bank**

**Banks vs NBFCs**

• UBS prefers **Banks over NBFCs** in oil shock scenario
• Banks have **stronger balance sheets and lower funding risk**

**UBS Preferred Banking Picks**

• **Axis Bank – 1.7x FY27E PBV**
• **Kotak Mahindra Bank – 1.6x FY27E PBV**

**Other Buy Rated Banks**

• **ICICI Bank – 2.2x PBV**
• **HDFC Bank – 1.8x PBV**
• **Bank of Baroda – 1.0x PBV**
• **Canara Bank – 1.0x PBV**

**EPS Sensitivity to Macro Risks**

• **Loan Growth −1 ppt → EPS impact −0.1% to −1.7%**
• **NIM −5 bps → EPS impact −1.3% to −3.8%**
• **Non-interest income −2% → EPS impact −0.9% to −3.8%**
• **Credit cost +5 bps → EPS impact −1.1% to −2.8%**

**Most Resilient Banks**

• **ICICI Bank (EPS impact ~−3.9%)**
• **Kotak Mahindra Bank (~−4.8%)**
• **Axis Bank (~−5.0%)**

**More Sensitive Banks**

• **IDFC First Bank (~−8.3%)**
• **Bandhan Bank (~−7.9%)**
• **IndusInd Bank (~−7.8%)**

**Key Takeaway for Investors**

• **Mild oil shock → Limited banking sector impact**
• **Sharp oil spike → Consumption slowdown & margin pressure**
• **Large private banks remain structurally stronger**

**Strategy**

• **Axis Bank & Kotak Bank for moderate oil risk**
• **ICICI Bank for high oil shock scenario**
• **Private Banks likely to outperform PSU Banks & NBFCs**

**Global Capital & Commodity Trends – Second Order Effects Of Middle East War**

**Agri Commodity Cycle Opportunity**
• Rising Oil Prices Can Trigger **Second Order Inflation In Agri Commodities**
• Fertilizers, Logistics And Farm Inputs Become Costlier
• Similar Pattern Seen During **Russia–Ukraine War Cycle**
• Agri Commodity Cycles Typically **Last Longer Once Started**

• ⛽ 30-Day Waiver: US Treasury under Scott Bessent grants temporary 30-day waiver for Indian refiners to purchase Russian oil.

• 🚢 Limited Scope: Waiver applies only to Russian oil already stranded at sea, limiting financial benefit to Russia.

• 🤝 Strategic Message: US expects India to increase purchases of US oil in the longer term.

Temporary relief helps Indian refiners maintain crude supply and reduce immediate energy price pressure.

US stocks closed lower as rising oil prices may raise fears of inflation and may hurt economic growth. Dow fell 1.61%, S&P dropped 0.56%, Nasdaq closed lower by 0.26% and the Small cap Russell 2000 lost almost 1.90%. 

USD index rallied back above 99.05, 10 year bond yields jumped above 4.14% on doubts over future rate cuts due to rising crude prices stroking higher inflation and cboe vix jumped above 23.75. 

Crude prices moved higher, escalating tensions in Middle East and disruption in shipping route at the Strait of Hormuz added to risk premium on the prices of oil. WTI traded near 79 rising close to 5.50%, at the peak of the day the benchmark topped 81 before settling below 79, Brent on the other hand had a measured move rising less than 3.00% to 83.75. 

Indian markets snapped 3 days of losses helped by gains in Metals, Defence names and rally in market heavyweights like RIL and HDFC Bank. Sensex rallied 900 points and Nifty added 285 points to close above 24750. SMID names also saw a small comeback on the back of better equity sentiment. 

*Weak Wall Street cues,  rising trio of crude, USD index and US yields may lead to some pullback in Indian markets when Dalal Street opens for trade. Participants may however watch out for stability post gap down open near this week lows and look at opportunities in beaten down names in sectors like IT for near term trade. Also avoid chasing war beneficiaries like Defence and Metals to allow some cooling effect in their stocks before putting additional money to work.*

(3.43 am IST)

Maruti Suzuki to Expand Capacity to Meet Rising Demand

Maruti Suzuki plans to increase annual production capacity by 500,000 units from next fiscal year to meet strong domestic demand.

The company currently has installed capacity of about 2.6 million units per year across plants in Haryana and Gujarat.

New plants will add ~250,000 units annually each, with capacity additions happening in phases.

The Kharkhoda (Haryana) plant is expected to start production in Q1 of next fiscal, while the fourth line at Hansalpur (Gujarat) may begin in Q2.

Maruti currently has a large order backlog of around 200,000 vehicles.

Dealer inventory is tight at ~12 days, significantly lower than the ideal level of about 30 days.

The company produces 220,000–230,000 vehicles per month, with 170,000–180,000 units for domestic sales and ~50,000 units for exports.

Exports to Africa, the Middle East and other markets are also expected to grow with higher production capacity.

MRF to Invest ₹5,300 Cr in New Tyre Plant in Tamil Nadu

MRF plans to set up a greenfield tyre manufacturing plant in Tamil Nadu with an investment of ~₹5,300 crore.

The plant will be located at Sipcot Industrial Park in Sivaganga district.

The project will manufacture automotive tyres and related products.

The investment will be implemented over ~12 years and is expected to create around 1,000 direct jobs.

The company has signed a non-binding MoU with the Tamil Nadu government through its investment facilitation agency.

MRF currently operates four manufacturing units in India across Goa, Telangana, and Gujarat.

The project is subject to land allocation, infrastructure support, and regulatory approvals from the state government.

BofA Says Oil Spike Biggest Risk to U.S. Economy

• ⚠️ Key Risk Identified: Bank of America says sharp rise in oil prices is the main potential threat to the U.S. economy.

• 🌍 Conflict Impact Limited: The U.S.–Iran conflict has not materially changed the economic outlook so far.

• 🛢️ Inflation Effect: A $10 increase in crude could raise inflation by ~0.1% and reduce GDP growth by ~0.1%.

• ⏳ Short-Term Impact: Economic effects usually fade within a year as higher energy prices reduce demand.

• 🇺🇸 Lower Vulnerability: The U.S. is now a net exporter of oil and gas, making it less exposed to oil shocks than before.

🟡 Impact: Neutral
Oil price spikes could temporarily affect inflation and growth, but the U.S. economy is structurally more resilient to energy shocks.

reports.text:
Compressors, Pumps & Diesel Engines: The ICE Resurrection  

Did you think diesel and ICE tech were history? Q3 FY26 proves otherwise — India’s heavy engineering sector is witnessing a diesel comeback.  

The ICE Vacuum:  
- As global OEMs chase EVs, they’re exiting traditional engine manufacturing.  
- This exit has created a supply vacuum now filled by Indian firms, turning ICE into a high-margin, defensible niche.  

AI’s Hidden Power Driver:  
- Hyperscale data centers demand 99.999% uptime — far beyond what current batteries can support.  
- Result: Surging orders for high-kVA diesel gensets as backup for AI infrastructure.  

China+1 & Export Momentum:  
- Western buyers are shifting RFQs for Stage-3 and Stage-4 engines to India.  
- U.S. import duty cuts (50% → 18%) are further boosting cost competitiveness of Indian heavy-engine exports.  

The Solar Pump Trap:  
- PM-KUSUM solar pump projects deliver big volumes but weak margins.  
- Fixed-price contracts and volatile input costs (copper, steel) strain working capital and profitability.  

Key Takeaway:  
As EV hype peaks, Indian manufacturers with strong ICE, genset, and heavy-engine portfolios are quietly becoming the new global powerhouses.

- Impact: Neutral to Slight Negative.  

Key Takeaway: GST authorities raised certain ITC-related issues, but GK Energy expects limited financial or operational consequences as it seeks legal recourse.

D2C OUTPACES MARKETPLACES | MCKINSEY REPORT  

Growth Projections  
- D2C market: $10-12B today → $55-60B by 2030 (38% CAGR).  
- Quick commerce: $5-6B → $35-40B (45% CAGR).  
- Overall e-commerce: $70-80B → $180-200B; marketplaces ~$100B (10-12% CAGR).  

Key Drivers  
- D2C 3x faster than marketplaces; ~50% incremental GMV from MSMEs outside platforms.  
- Rising internet penetration, incomes, Tier-2/3 demand.  
- E-commerce penetration low: 6-8% vs 23-25% (US), 25-27% (China).  

Strategic Shift  
- MSMEs prefer own websites/social (53%) for lower fees, data control, margins.  

Key Takeaway:  
D2C/MSME-led unbundling accelerates e-commerce to $200B by 2030 — platforms cede ground to direct brands in India's low-penetration, high-potential market.

RBI PROJECT FINANCE NORMS IMPACT  

Key Restrictions (Effective Oct 1, 2025)  
- Debt:Equity ratio fixed at sanction — no top-up loans allowed during project life.  
- Lenders cannot fund cost overruns/delays beyond approved amount.  
- Aims for developer "skin in the game" and financial discipline.  

Real Estate Implications  
- Banks, NBFCs, HFCs face reduced lending flexibility for projects.  
- Higher risks for lenders on under-construction realty exposures.  

Market Adjustments  
- Discretionary funding slows; enhanced transparency/monitoring expected.  
- Workaround: GCP loans with strict RBI end-use compliance.  

Key Takeaway:  
RBI's rigid project finance rules curb mid-project lending, pressuring real estate developers and lender balance sheets — favour completed projects over under-construction plays.

Infosys–Intel Tie-Up to Accelerate Enterprise AI Adoption

Infosys and Intel announced a strategic partnership to help enterprises scale AI from pilot projects to full production deployments.

The collaboration will combine Intel’s high-performance computing platforms with Infosys Topaz and AI services.

Solutions will be optimized for Intel Xeon processors, Intel Gaudi AI accelerators, and Intel AI PCs.

The partnership aims to create open standards across the edge-to-cloud AI ecosystem.

Focus areas include model management, data integration, performance monitoring, and built-in security features.

The companies will jointly design, develop, and benchmark enterprise AI workloads tailored to business needs.

AI solutions will support IT operations, developer productivity, and automation-led workflows.

The platform will also enable AI agents that can access enterprise data and coordinate tasks within governance frameworks.

 India Proposes Stricter Emission Norms for Tractors & Construction Equipment 

•  New Draft Norms: Ministry of Road Transport and Highways proposes TREM Stage V emission norms for agricultural machinery and tighter CEV Stage V norms for construction equipment.

•  Stricter Pollution Limits: Particulate matter limits to be reduced by ~40% for tractor engines (56–560 kW).

•  Advanced Technology Needed: Manufacturers must now control both particle mass and particle count, pushing adoption of advanced emission technologies.

• Implementation Timeline: Proposed to be effective from October 1, 2026, after a 30-day public consultation period.

 Impact: Mixed
Cleaner emission standards support environmental goals, but may increase manufacturing costs for tractor and construction equipment makers.

JM || Adani Energy Solutions 

Unlocking the Gridlock

We believe AESL is strongly positioned to benefit from India’s T&D growth story supported by a robust INR 778bn transmission order book, 24.6mn smart metering portfolio and a stable distribution franchise with a regulated asset base (RAB) of INR 96bn. 

We estimate revenue (ex-SCA) /EBITDA /PAT would compound at 19%/15%/50% over FY25–28E driven by commissioning and smart meter scale-up. 

We value the company on an SotP basis, valuing transmission at 13x EV/EBITDA, smart metering at 12x EV/EBITDA and distribution at 2.5x P/B—all on FY28 estimates. Initiate at BUY with a TP of INR 1,199 (potential upside of 19%).

**Oil at 8-Month High – India Monitoring Supplies**

**Middle East Tensions Impact Oil**
• Crude prices hit **8-month high** amid Iran–Israel conflict
• Shipping slowed near **Strait of Hormuz** after Iranian warnings

**Critical Route for India**
• **~50% of India’s crude & LPG imports** pass through Hormuz
• Government sets up **24×7 control room** to monitor fuel supplies

**Current Supply Position**
• India has **adequate stocks of petrol, diesel & ATF**
• Total **commercial + strategic reserves ~100 million barrels**
• Covers roughly **40–45 days of crude imports**

**Key Risk Area**
• **Natural gas & LPG supply risk higher**
• India heavily dependent on **Qatar LNG shipments via Hormuz**

**Macro Impact if Crisis Prolongs**
• Higher **oil prices, freight & war-risk insurance**
• Could increase **import bill and inflation pressure**

**Import Context**
• India crude import bill **$137B in FY25**
• **$100.4B already spent in first 10 months** of current fiscal year

Singtel to Cut Stake as Airtel Simplifies Promoter Structure

Bharti Airtel plans to consolidate promoter holdings under Bharti Telecom (BTL) as part of a restructuring roadmap.

Bharti Telecom currently holds ~41% stake in Airtel, making it the main promoter vehicle.

About 7% stake held by Singtel and ~1% held by the Mittal family outside BTL will be gradually reduced via market transactions over the next 3–4 years.

After restructuring, Bharti Telecom will remain the sole direct promoter entity with 41% stake in Airtel.

Singtel and the Mittal family will pare their residual direct holdings to zero, increasing the public float.

The plan is part of a broader promoter restructuring first outlined in August 2022 to simplify and equalise holdings.

Overall promoter shareholding in Airtel has already declined from 55.9% (June 2022) to about 48.9% currently.

**Key Takeaway**

**BOFA GLOBAL RESEARCH – India Trucking Cycle**

**Industry Outlook**
• India CV industry entering **new upcycle after ~7 years**
• **M&HCV volumes expected ~10% CAGR**
• Demand recovery visible beyond small CV segment

**Key Demand Drivers**
• Return of **small fleet operators** as financing improves
• **Strong freight market** with higher rentals & utilization
• **Replacement cycle overdue** (fleet age **10+ years vs 7–8 earlier**)
• Aging **BS4 trucks reducing used vehicle attractiveness**

**Industry Structure**
• Highly consolidated market structure
• **Tata Motors, Ashok Leyland, VECV** control **~80% share**
• Strong pricing discipline, service network & brand stickiness
• High entry barriers for new players

**Regulatory Tailwinds**
• Focus on **safety, braking systems, ADAS & better cabins**
• Manageable vehicle price inflation
• **Electrification risk low for heavy trucks for ~5 years**

**Profitability Outlook**
• Industry EBITDA margins expected in **mid-teens**
• **Ashok Leyland:** targeting mid-teen margins
• **Tata Motors CV:** teen EBITDA margins expected

**Structural Growth Drivers**
• **Exports growth** in Middle East, Africa & SAARC
• **LCV demand** supported by e-commerce & last-mile logistics
• **Bus demand** rising via replacement programs & **PM e-Bus Sewa**

**Market Structure Insights**

**M&HCV Segment**
• **95% share with three players**
• Tata Motors – Leader
• Ashok Leyland – #2
• VECV – #3

**LCV Segment**
• More fragmented market
• Leaders: **Mahindra & Mahindra, Tata Motors, Ashok Leyland, Maruti Suzuki**

**Industry Economics**
• CV industry **ROE ~25–30%**
• Stable margins vs other auto segments
• Strong profitability visibility

**BofA Investment View – Top Picks**
• **Tata Motors (CV business)**
• **Ashok Leyland**

**Investor Takeaway**
• Industry entering **multi-year trucking upcycle**
• Driven by **replacement demand, freight strength & consolidation**
• Strong earnings visibility for CV OEMs and suppliers 📈

**CITI ON OIL & GAS – MIDDLE EAST CONFLICT IMPACT**

**Gas Value Chain Risks**  
- Gas segment faces higher risks than oil from conflict disruptions.  
- Qatar supplies 40-50% of India's LNG imports; tough to replace amid global gas price surge.

**Company-Specific Gas Exposures**  
- Petronet: Elevated volume risk from LNG supply constraints.  
- GAIL: Gas transmission volumes potentially at risk.  
- Gujarat Gas: Higher vulnerability among CGDs due to heavy Qatar and spot LNG reliance.

**Oil Side Dynamics**  
- Upstream (ONGC): Benefits from elevated oil prices, barring windfall tax revival.  
- OMCs: Margin pressures from rising crude costs.  
- RIL: Gains in O2C from stronger refining margins, especially diesel cracks.

**Key Takeaway**  
Gas importers and midstream face acute supply risks; favor upstream oil plays like ONGC amid price upside potential.

**ICICI DIRECT – RACL Geartech (RAUINT)**

**Investment View**
• **BUY** | CMP **₹1,340** | TP **₹1,615**
• Upside **~21% (12M)**
• Valuation: **25x FY28E P/E**

**Business Positioning**
• Premium precision gears & safety-critical components
• Export-led model: **~70% exports (Q3FY26)**
• FY25 geography: Europe **52%**, India/APAC **46%**
• Segment mix: 2W **39%**, CV **17%**, PV **13%**, ATV/RTV **13%**

**Q3FY26 Performance**
• Revenue **₹137.3 cr (+22% YoY, +14% QoQ)**
• EBITDA **₹31.3 cr (+31% YoY)** | Margin **22.8%**
• PAT **₹16.3 cr (+149% YoY, +35% QoQ)**
• Strong earnings acceleration & margin recovery

**Growth Drivers & Orderbook**
• FY27 revenue guidance: **~₹565 cr (+15–20%)**
• BMW EV ramp-up, premium 2W OEM scaling, KTM recovery
• US truck EPS components via ZF
• Multi-year premium orderbook; sales visibility **~₹1,000 cr by FY29–30E**

**Financial Trajectory (FY25–28E)**
• Revenue CAGR **~17.8%**
• EBITDA CAGR **~22.7%**
• PAT CAGR **~47.6%**
• EBITDA margin **19.9% → 22.5%**
• PAT margin **5.7% → 11.2%**

**Balance Sheet & Capex**
• FY27 capex **₹77 cr** (efficiency + selective expansion)
• Debt/EBITDA **3.7x → 1.7x** (FY25–28E)
• Debt/Equity **1.3x → 0.5x**

**Valuation Comfort**
• FY28E P/E **20.7x** | EV/EBITDA **12x**
• Improving margins + deleveraging support re-rating potential

**Investor Takeaway**
• Strong export-led niche franchise
• Multi-year order visibility
• Earnings compounding story with improving balance sheet

**BHARTI AIRTEL – JM Financial Deep Dive (1 Mar 2026)**

**Rating & Valuation**
• Maintain **BUY**
• Target Price at **₹2,455** (~**30.7% upside**)
• FY28 EV/EBITDA at TP: **10.1x (consol.)**
• SoTP fair value at **₹3,120** (14% IRR)

**Core Thesis**
• **14–15% EBITDA CAGR** over next 2–3 years
• **10–11% ARPU CAGR** expected
• Implied FY28 FCF yield ~**5.5%**

**Key Concerns Addressed**

• **NBFC Foray**
• Equity commitment capped at **₹200 bn**
• First-year spend only **₹15–20 bn**
• Consolidated FCF at **₹600–700 bn p.a.**
• Seen as natural adjacency leveraging **370 mn+** customer base

• **Promoter Stake Overhang**
• Holding reduced to ~**48.9%**
• ~**8.5%** more likely to be offloaded gradually
• Promoters aim to move back toward **50%** over time

• **Tariff Hike Timing**
• Base case: hike within next **6 months**
• Delay to CY27 = only **3–4% EBITDA downside**
• ARPU needs to reach **₹295–325 by FY28** for healthy RoCE

**Financial Trajectory (FY24–28)**
• Revenue: **₹1,514 bn → ₹2,717 bn**
• EBITDA: **₹797 bn → ₹1,491 bn** (margin ~55%)
• PAT: **₹138 bn → ₹598 bn**
• ROE: **17.2% → 33.4%**

**Sensitivity**
• ₹10 ARPU change = **₹45/share** valuation impact
• 10 mn subs change = **₹35/share** impact

**Investor View**
• Structural ARPU re-rating story
• Limited downside in worst-case tariff delay
• Strong FCF + compounding earnings profile

🚀 **KESAR PETROPRODUCTS – Turnaround to Structural Growth Story**

🔹 **Shift from Crude to High-Margin Pigments**
Moved from low-margin CPC crude (1–2%) to downstream pigments (15–18% margins).
9M FY26:
• EBITDA up ~74% YoY
• PAT up ~85% YoY
• EBITDA margin crossed 16%

**Clear operating leverage visible.**

🔹 **Waste-to-Wealth Fertilizer Integration**
Converting pigment by-products into complex fertilizers.
Commercial production started.
At ~75% utilization, fertilizer alone can add 20–25% additional revenue.

🔹 **Co-Generation Plant = Structural Cost Savings**
50% coal reduction.
₹3–4 Cr+ annual savings expected.
Margin kicker from FY27.

🔹 **Strong Guidance**
• 100% bottom-line growth target for FY26
• 15–16% EBITDA margin
• 20% topline growth next year
• 18–20% CAGR over 3 years

🔹 **Promoter Confidence**
₹38 Cr capital infusion via warrants to support expansion.

**📌 Big Picture:**
The company is transitioning from survival to structured growth better product mix, stronger margins, integrated ecosystem.
